name: Route of the Walled Cities (GR-80)
name_original: Yacimiento de Siega Verde – circular desde Ciudad Rodrigo
description: This moderate 31.3-mile mountain bike route from Ciudad Rodrigo explores the Águeda River and the ancient Siega Verde Archaeological Site.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Ciudad Rodrigo offers a diverse landscape characterized by the Águeda River, the Sierra de Gata mountains, and extensive holm oak dehesas. The region features varied terrain, including river valleys, forested areas, and open pastures, with notable rock formations in the Sierra de Gata. This environment provides a robust network of mountain bike trails suitable for exploring both natural beauty and historical sites. Best mountain bike trails around Ciudad Rodrigo The most popular mountain bike trail is Route of the Walled Cities (GR-80), a 31.3 miles (50.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 9 minutes to complete. This moderate route connects Ciudad Rodrigo with Almeida in Portugal, passing by the ancient Siega Verde Archaeological Site. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Town Hall – Plaza Mayor Ciudad Rodrigo loop from Ciudad Rodrigo, a moderate 20.4 miles (32.9 km) path. This route offers a scenic loop around the city, incorporating views of the historic center and surrounding river landscape. Local mountain bikers also love the Plaza Mayor Ciudad Rodrigo – Cathedral of Santa María loop from Ciudad Rodrigo, a 30.2 miles (48.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 44 minutes. Mountain biking around Ciudad Rodrigo is defined by the Águeda River, the Sierra de Gata, and holm oak dehesas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging mountain paths. The routes in Ciudad Rodrigo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 14 reviews. More than 130 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Ciudad Rodrigo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of mountain bike trails can I expect around Ciudad Rodrigo?
A: The region offers a diverse range of mountain bike trails, from river valleys along the Águeda River to forested areas within the Sierra de Gata and open holm oak dehesas. You'll find varied terrain, including challenging climbs and scenic views, suitable for different skill levels. The Sierra de Gata MTB Centre alone features 10 distinct routes covering 233 km of signposted paths.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Ciudad Rodrigo?
A: While the majority of routes listed are moderate, the Sierra de Gata MTB Centre is designed to cater to various skill levels, including those suitable for all-terrain biking. These centers often provide services and information to help you choose the best route for your group. For a scenic loop around the city with moderate difficulty, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20141188" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Town Hall – Plaza Mayor Ciudad Rodrigo loop from Ciudad Rodrigo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see while mountain biking near Ciudad Rodrigo?
A: Many routes incorporate historical and natural sights. You can cycle past the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3984587" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor Ciudad Rodrigo&lt;/a&gt;, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4527228" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cathedral of Santa María&lt;/a&gt;, or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4527256" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Enrique II of Trastámara&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994057707/route-of-the-walled-cities-gr-80" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Walled Cities (GR-80)&lt;/a&gt; also passes by the UNESCO World Heritage-listed Siega Verde Archaeological Site, known for its prehistoric rock art.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, several popular routes are designed as loops, offering a convenient way to explore the area. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20141188" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Town Hall – Plaza Mayor Ciudad Rodrigo loop from Ciudad Rodrigo&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20141232" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor Ciudad Rodrigo – Cathedral of Santa María loop from Ciudad Rodrigo&lt;/a&gt;, both providing varied terrain and scenic views.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Ciudad Rodrigo?
A: The region's climate generally allows for mountain biking through much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes, making them ideal. Summers can be hot, especially in open areas, while winters are typically mild but can have cooler, wetter periods. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Ciudad Rodrigo?
A: The mountain biking routes around Ciudad Rodrigo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 14 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, the combination of natural beauty with historical sites, and the well-maintained paths that cater to various skill levels.

Q: Are there services available for mountain bikers in the region?
A: Yes, the Sierra de Gata MTB Centre, an IMBA-approved facility, provides essential services for cyclists. These include information points, repair tools, and washing facilities. Additionally, the Tourist Hostel in the Sierra de Gata offers workshops and bike rentals, making it convenient for visitors.

Q: Can I find challenging mountain bike routes in Ciudad Rodrigo?
A: Absolutely. While many routes are moderate, the Sierra de Gata region is known for its more challenging climbs and technical sections, offering rewarding experiences for advanced riders. Out of the 19 mountain bike tours in the area, 3 are classified as difficult, providing a good test for experienced cyclists.

Q: Are there any routes that connect Ciudad Rodrigo to other towns or regions?
A: Yes, a notable route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994057707/route-of-the-walled-cities-gr-80" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Walled Cities (GR-80)&lt;/a&gt;. This 50.3 km moderate trail connects Ciudad Rodrigo with Almeida in Portugal, offering an international cycling experience that blends natural beauty with rich cultural heritage.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on the trails?
A: The trails often follow the scenic Águeda River, featuring a historic Roman bridge. In the Sierra de Gata, you'll ride through dense woods of chestnut, oak, and pine trees, encounter striking rock formations, and pass by numerous streams. The region also boasts crystal-clear natural pools, perfect for a refreshing stop, and extensive holm oak dehesas.

Q: Is it possible to find mountain bike trails with good viewpoints?
A: Many routes around Ciudad Rodrigo offer excellent scenic views. The historic walls of Ciudad Rodrigo itself provide magnificent panoramic vistas of the surrounding verdant landscapes. Additionally, the mountainous terrain of the Sierra de Gata region is renowned for its scenic viewpoints along its biking routes.
tips: The Plaza Mayor is the center of the city. All activity has always developed around it, in it you can still enjoy interesting buildings.

The zoomorphic carvings of Siega Verde occupy more than a kilometer of stones on the banks of the Águeda and are the most important open-air site on the Iberian Peninsula. They are part of the Vale de Coa complex, in Portugal, which was declared a World Heritage Site by UNESCO.

Horses, bears, goats and other species extinct on the peninsula such as the woolly rhinoceros, reindeer and bison are represented in the carvings. The site is fenced for its preservation, but an interpretation room has been built that details the life of the inhabitants of the area in the Palaeolithic.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Yacimiento de Siega Verde – circular desde Ciudad Rodrigo mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This mountain bike route is approximately 50.3 km long and typically takes around 4 hours and 9 minutes to complete. This duration is for the riding time itself, so plan for additional time to explore the archaeological site or enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain on this route is varied, primarily consisting of unpaved paths, natural surfaces, and gravel sections. You will also encounter some asphalt and paved roads. The trail follows the banks of the Águeda River, offering picturesque scenery and rock formations.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginner mountain bikers?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is classified as 'moderate' and requires good fitness and advanced riding skills. It corresponds approximately to STS S2 difficulty, meaning it's more challenging than a beginner-level trail.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The most significant highlight is the Siega Verde Archaeological Site, a UNESCO World Heritage site featuring over 440 ancient Paleolithic rock engravings along the Águeda River. The route also starts and ends near Ciudad Rodrigo, a historic walled city.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike the Yacimiento de Siega Verde – circular desde Ciudad Rodrigo trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail can be ridden year-round, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking. Summers can be hot, and winters might bring colder, potentially wetter conditions, which could affect trail surfaces.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: Information regarding specific dog restrictions for this exact route is not explicitly provided. However, when cycling in natural areas, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure their safety, especially near archaeological sites or in agricultural zones.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to ride this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: Riding the mountain bike route itself does not typically require a permit or entrance fee. However, if you plan to visit the Siega Verde Archaeological Site, there may be an entrance fee or guided tour requirement to access the rock art viewing areas. It's recommended to check the site's official website for current visitor information.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail near Ciudad Rodrigo?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting and ending near Ciudad Rodrigo. Parking options would typically be available within or on the outskirts of Ciudad Rodrigo. Look for public parking areas in the town, especially near the historic center, to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Does this mountain bike route intersect with any other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other official trails. Significant portions of it overlap with the 'Ruta de las Ciudades Amuralladas' (46%) and 'Ciudades Amuralladas 3: Saelices el Chico-Ciudad Rodrigo' (32%). Smaller sections also coincide with 'Ciudades Amuralladas 2: Villar de Ciervo-Saelices el Chico' (14%) and 'Camino Torres - 01 Salamanca to Almeida' (2%).

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter along the Águeda River?
faqs.answer: Along the Águeda River, you might encounter various bird species, and potentially small mammals common to riverine and Mediterranean scrub habitats. The area's natural beauty and rock formations provide diverse ecosystems for local fauna.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or water stops along the trail?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Ciudad Rodrigo, where you'll find a full range of amenities including cafes, restaurants, and shops. Along the trail itself, especially in more remote sections, amenities will be scarce, so it's essential to carry enough water and snacks for the duration of your ride.
